rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings only.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers that are not present in the AADL model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, ensure type consistency between expressions and their declarations.
Only reference visible AADL features or declared helpers within the same annex block.
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for constants and equation declarations.
Validate that all references in AGREE contracts resolve to existing AADL elements or declared variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level assignments should be placed in component implementation scopes.
Equation declarations in AGREE must have consistent types on both sides of the '=' operator.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scopes; use assign for output bindings.
Do not reference identifiers that are not declared or visible in the current AADL scope.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs implementations
Component type contracts and their scope rules
